message:
fix(pthread): add FORCE_NATIVE_MUTEX flag to bypass host delegation in tests

The pthread_mutex_core_test had 2 persistent failures because all 5
mutex operations (init/destroy/lock/trylock/unlock) unconditionally
delegate to host glibc via dlvsym(RTLD_NEXT) when running as a normal
test binary. The native futex state machine was never exercised, so:

- futex_mutex_unlock_without_lock_is_eperm failed because glibc's
  default (non-ERRORCHECK) mutex silently accepts unlock without lock
- futex_mutex_contention_increments_wait_and_wake_counters timed out
  because branch counters were never incremented (glibc handled all ops)

Added FORCE_NATIVE_MUTEX AtomicBool that pthread_mutex_reset_state_for_tests()
sets to true. When set, mutex operations skip the host_pthread_mutex_*_fn()
delegation and fall through to the native futex implementation. This lets
tests exercise the futex lock/unlock/trylock state machine directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
